message:
feat(server): add HTTP liveness probe supervisor and hardened listener config

Core HTTP server hardening with automatic crash/stall recovery:

- Add run_http_headless_supervisor() to wrap headless HTTP (serve --no-tui)
  under the same supervised restart policy as the TUI path, enabling
  self-healing from transport starvation or listener crashes
- Implement probe_http_healthz() liveness probe that performs raw TCP
  health checks against /healthz with configurable timeout (300ms)
- Add normalized_probe_host() to map wildcard bind addresses (0.0.0.0,
  ::, [::]) to their loopback equivalents for probing
- Introduce hardened_http_listener_config() using Http1ListenerConfig:
  disable keep-alive, limit to 1 request per connection, enforce idle
  timeout (10s), cap headers at 64KB, body at 16MB, and set max
  connections to 4096 with 5s drain timeout
- Replace single-threaded HTTP runtime with resolve_http_runtime_worker_threads()
  that auto-scales 2..16 workers based on available parallelism and the
  AM_HTTP_WORKER_THREADS env override
- Add exponential backoff restart logic (200ms..5s) via restart_backoff_ms()
  and reset_probe_state() shared between headless and TUI supervisors
- Integrate liveness probes into the TUI supervisor's timeout path:
  after 3 consecutive probe failures (2s interval, 15s startup grace),
  automatically force-restart the HTTP server instance
- Reset probe state after transport switches (toggle/set transport base)
  to avoid false positives during reconfiguration
- Add normalized_probe_host_maps_wildcards_to_loopback unit test
- Update imports: add Http1ListenerConfig, Http1Config, std::io::{Read,Write},
  std::net::ToSocketAddrs

Test timing budget relaxation (tui_events.rs):
- Relax performance test budgets for debug builds to prevent false failures
  on shared/dev hosts under CPU contention
- iter_recent benchmark: 10ms release / 30ms debug (was 10ms for both)
- Initial refresh benchmark: 50ms release / 200ms debug (was 50ms for both)
- Include budget value in assertion messages for easier diagnostics

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
